pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: High-level introduction to bitcold: what it is, its purpose as a Bitcoin cold wallet CLI, key design principles (offline-first, air-gapped), and a map of the major subsystems. Links to child pages for details.

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: Installation, build process, environment variables (BITCOLD_BITCOIN_NETWORK, BITCOLD_PASSPHRASE), and the first-run passphrase setup flow. Covers bin/run.js entrypoint and tsconfig/build pipeline.

pages.title: CLI Command Reference
pages.purpose: Complete reference for all bitcold commands: wallet and tx namespaces, their arguments, options, and expected outputs. Summarizes the full command tree and links to deeper subsystem pages.

pages.title: Architecture Overview
pages.purpose: Describes the layered architecture of bitcold: CLI layer → Domain layer → Persistence layer. Explains how data flows from user input to signed transaction output. Links to child pages for each layer.

pages.title: CLI Layer
pages.purpose: Details the CLI layer: how commander is used to define commands, the printer output utility, the logger, and the cli-level error handling pattern. Covers src/cli/ directory structure.

pages.title: Domain Layer
pages.purpose: Explains the domain model: Wallet, Account, BtcAddress, and Transaction classes. Covers how domain objects are constructed, serialized.

pages.title: Persistence Layer
pages.purpose: Covers the encrypted storage system: EncryptedUserHomeJsonStorage, WalletRepository, the ~/.bitcold directory, file permissions (0o700), lazy-loading cache, and the read-modify-write cycle for wallet CRUD.

pages.title: Wallet Management
pages.purpose: Overview of all wallet lifecycle operations: creation, display, listing, renaming, removal, and account management. Links to child pages for each major operation group.

pages.title: Wallet Creation and Import
pages.purpose: Deep dive into the wallet create command: mnemonic generation, entropy-bits input, BIP39 passphrase, ephemeral mode, alias validation, and the Wallet.generateWithDefaultAccount() factory flow.

pages.title: Account Management
pages.purpose: Explains the wallet account add/remove sub-commands, BIP44 derivation index assignment, alias generation (account_n pattern), and the constraint preventing removal of the last account.

pages.title: Wallet Display and Listing
pages.purpose: Covers wallet show (with --private and --mnemonic flags), wallet list (with locked/unlocked summary), and the show() helper function that formats wallet output including derivation paths and addresses.

pages.title: Transaction Signing
pages.purpose: Overview of the tx sign command pipeline: UTXO input parsing, address resolution (wallet@account refs:addressindex), PSBT construction, signing with ECPair/secp256k1, fee-rate calculation, and QR code output. Links to child pages.

pages.title: PSBT Construction and Signing
pages.purpose: Technical details of how UnsignedBtcTransaction (PSBT) is built from UTXOs, how change outputs are calculated, how BtcAddress.sign() uses ECPairFactory and tiny-secp256k1 to sign all inputs, and how SignedBtcTransaction.hex() produces the final raw transaction.

pages.title: Address Resolution and Validation
pages.purpose: Explains the wallet@account address reference syntax, how resolveAddress() and dereferenceAddress() look up BtcAddress objects from the repository, and the validator utilities (isNotValidBtcAddressOrRef, isAddressRef, isNotValidHex, etc.).

pages.title: Cryptographic Subsystems
pages.purpose: Overview of all cryptographic primitives used in bitcold: AES-256-GCM encryption, scrypt KDF, BIP32/BIP84 HD derivation, BIP39 mnemonic generation, and Base58 encoding. Links to child pages for each subsystem.

pages.title: AES-256-GCM Encryption and scrypt KDF
pages.purpose: Details the EncryptedEnvelopeV1 format, aesEncrypt/aesDecrypt functions, scrypt key derivation parameters, the AesAuthenticationError, and how the envelope is stored as JSON on disk.

pages.title: HD Wallet Key Derivation (BIP32/BIP84/BIP39)
pages.purpose: Explains the BTC_DERIVATION_PATH_PREFIX (m/84'/0'/0'/0), the derive() function that produces privateKey (hex+WIF), publicKey, and P2WPKH address, mnemonic generation from word count or raw entropy buffer, and the bip32/bip39/tiny-secp256k1 dependency chain.

pages.title: Security Model
pages.purpose: Comprehensive explanation of bitcold's security design: CLI-level passphrase protecting the storage, per-wallet mnemonic passphrase (25th word), AES-256-GCM authenticated encryption, directory permissions, and the air-gap workflow.

pages.title: CLI Passphrase and Storage Encryption
pages.purpose: Details the ensureCliLevelSecretInitialized() flow: first-run passphrase creation with confirmation, subsequent passphrase prompting, BITCOLD_PASSPHRASE env var bypass, and how the wrong passphrase is detected via AES-GCM auth tag failure.

pages.title: Wallet Mnemonic Passphrase (BIP39 25th Word)
pages.purpose: Explains the per-wallet mnemonic passphrase: it is prompted at each wallet operation with confirmation when non-empty, then used directly for BIP39 seed derivation. The passphrase is never persisted and there is no correctness verification — an incorrect passphrase will silently derive different addresses. Users must verify the resulting address matches their expectation.

pages.title: Utilities and Shared Libraries
pages.purpose: Overview of shared utility modules: input validators, functional helpers (toMap, identity), display formatters, and the libs/ directory containing reusable core/persistence/utils abstractions.

pages.title: Input Validation
pages.purpose: Documents all validator functions in src/utils/validator.mts: isNotValidBtcAddressOrRef (multi-network try-catch), isAddressRef, isNotValidBech32, isNotValidHex, isValidMnemonicLength, isNotInt, isNotNumber, ensureSingleWhiteSpace, and the ECC initialization side-effect.

pages.title: Functional Utilities and Display Helpers
pages.purpose: Covers the fp.mts utilities (toMap, identity) used for Map construction in Wallet, the display.mts helpers (addressSummary, accountSummary) for truncated address display, and the libs/utils/functional.mts equivalents.

pages.title: Testing Infrastructure
pages.purpose: Covers the E2E test suite: CliSandbox helper for spawning isolated CLI processes with temp HOME directories, tty-hook for intercepting prompts, the wallet-lifecycle test scenarios (cryptographic truth sets, security tests), and how BITCOLD_PASSPHRASE is used to bypass interactive prompts in tests.

pages.title: Glossary
pages.purpose: Definitions of all codebase-specific terms, Bitcoin protocol concepts, BIP standards, and abbreviations used throughout bitcold. Each entry includes code pointers to relevant files and functions.
